entry.title: Political Course Report:
entry.content:
The global initial public offering market has entered a structural paradigm shift — declining transaction volume but drastically expanding individual deal size.
How will the simultaneous listing of three tech giants reshape the capital market landscape?
~$3 Trillion

Total Valuation

Source: Bloomberg, May 2026

~$200 Billion

Estimated Fundraising

Based on 5% free float estimate

48%

Tech Weight in S&P 500

Source: BofA Michael Hartnett

Part 01 — Context

What Happened

The global IPO market in 2026 exhibits a counter-intuitive structural characteristic: while the overall number of deals decreased 15% year-over-year,
total capital raised surged 45% year-over-year, reaching $42.6 billion in the first quarter — a new high since 2022.
This extreme concentration of capital reached an unprecedented peak in the second half of 2026.

The “ AI Big Three”—SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic — have a combined valuation approaching $3 trillion.
More importantly, the listing windows for these three entities are highly overlapping and extremely tight — SpaceX is expected to list as early as mid-June,
Anthropic is targeting the fall, and OpenAI is aiming for year-end or possibly deferring to early 2027 — creating enormous absorption pressure on global public market liquidity.

Based on the commonly applied 5% free float standard in public markets, the cumulative fundraising from these three companies will approach
$200 billion, surpassing the total proceeds of all U.S. IPOs with a market cap above $50 million from 2022 through Q1 2026.
This figure alone demonstrates the historic scale of this IPO wave.

Company Expected Listing Date Estimated Fundraising Proposed Valuation Exchange S-1 Status
SpaceX (SPCX) June 12, 2026 $40-80 Billion $1.75-2 Trillion NASDAQ Publicly Filed
Anthropic Fall 2026 (October) $30-50 Billion $965 Billion NASDAQ Confidential Draft Submitted
OpenAI 2026 Q4 / Deferred to 2027 $20-40 Billion $850 Billion-$1.1 Trillion NASDAQ Not Officially Filed

Historical Perspective: Comparison with Largest IPOs in History

To understand the scale of this IPO wave, we can compare it with the largest IPOs in history.
Alibaba’s $25 billion IPO in 2014 set a record, but SpaceX alone could raise $40-80 billion,
and the three companies combined could approach $200 billion — this is unprecedented.

When three companies with a combined valuation approaching $3 trillion simultaneously rush into the public market, this is no longer an ordinary IPO cycle, but a structural event that will redefine the global capital market landscape.

— Compiled based on Bloomberg, KPMG, Dealogic data

Part 02 — The Players

The True Face of the Three Companies

On the surface, it appears that three companies are simultaneously rushing into the public market, but in reality, each has vastly different motivations, predicaments, and leverage.
Below are their core financial profiles.

Data Sources: SpaceX S-1 Filing (May 20, 2026), Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, TechStackIPO.com, The Information, Morningstar, NDTV Profit

Core Metric SpaceX (SPCX) Anthropic OpenAI
Proposed Valuation $1.75-2 Trillion $965 Billion $850 Billion-$1.1 Trillion
2025 Revenue $18.7 Billion $47 Billion (annualized) $13 Billion (estimated)
Net Profit/Loss -$4.94 Billion Extremely Thin Margins -$14 Billion (2026E)
Expected Listing Date June 12, 2026 Fall 2026 (October) 2026 Q4 / Deferred to 2027
S-1 Filing Status ✅ Publicly Filed • Confidential Draft Submitted • Not Officially Filed
Core Business Segments Space Launch, Starlink Communications, AI (xAI Merger) Enterprise LLM, Security & Compliance Systems Consumer Multimodal AI, API Ecosystem
Major Capital Backers Musk (42% Equity), Alphabet Amazon, Google, Sequoia Capital Microsoft, SoftBank, Thrive Capital

SpaceX

$1.75-2T

2025 Revenue

$18.7 Billion

Starlink Contribution

$11.4 Billion (61%)

2025 Net Loss

-$4.94 Billion

Cumulative Deficit

$41.3 Billion

SpaceX’s true story is far more complex than “ Musk’s space empire.” In February 2026, SpaceX formally merged with Elon Musk’s AI company xAI,
a decision that directly exposed a massive financial black hole hidden beneath the halo of space exploration.

To build the “ Colossus” super data center with 100,000 GPUs and gigawatt-level energy consumption, and to develop the Grok large model,
xAI burned through over $6 billion in 2025 and continued consuming $2.5 billion in Q1 2026.
This resulted in SpaceX recording a single-quarter GAAP net loss of $4.28 billion in Q1 2026.

(Source: SpaceX S-1 Filing, May 20, 2026; Morningstar, May 21, 2026)

The underlying driver for SpaceX’s urgency to push through this “ century IPO”(ticker SPCX) at a $1.75-2 trillion valuation in mid-June
is to leverage public market liquidity to quickly raise $40 to $80 billion in pure cash,
preventing xAI’s computing power hemorrhage from dragging down its core space and Starlink businesses.

OpenAI

$850 Billion-$1.1T

2025 Revenue

$13 Billion (est.)

2026E Loss

-$14 Billion

Long-term Leases

$1.4 Trillion

Weekly Active Users

900 Million

OpenAI’s predicament is a triple overlay: revenue falling short of expectations, computing costs spiraling out of control, and long-term debt mounting.
In 2026, the company repeatedly missed monthly revenue targets, with growth stagnating at 900 million weekly active users.

Moving from simple “ billion-scale text processing” to “ million-dimensional multimodal physical simulation,” computing expenditure has exceeded the carrying capacity of traditional VC.
Sora’s ROI is only 0.14, burning $15 million daily with total revenue of just $2.1 million. Even more lethal is the $1.4 trillion in long-term data center and server hard leases — legal obligations that must be fulfilled regardless of revenue performance.

(Source: The AI Journal, May 2026; RoboRhythms, May 22, 2026)

HSBC’s analysis indicates that even accounting for current revenue growth rates, OpenAI still needs to supplement over $207 billion in pure cash flow before 2030 to maintain basic operations,
otherwise it will face legal risks of computing power default.

Anthropic

$965 Billion

Q2 Revenue Run Rate

$10.9 Billion

SpaceX Lease

$15 Billion/Year

Series H Funding

$6.5 Billion

Expected Listing

October 2026

Anthropic has achieved great success in the enterprise market through “ Constitutional AI” and Project Glasswing,
with Q2 revenue run rate projected to reach a remarkable $10.9 billion.

However, it is similarly locked into extremely expensive computing supply chains. Beyond the hundreds of billions in computing and chip contracts signed with Google, Broadcom, and Amazon,
Anthropic signed an exceptionally onerous contract with SpaceX in May 2026:
leasing two orbital and ground data center clusters from SpaceX at $15 billion per year.

(Source: Anthropic Official Announcement, May 28, 2026; 36Kr, Jun 2, 2026)

This means that even if Anthropic conducts no R&D whatsoever, it faces $15 billion in rigid payment obligations to SpaceX alone each year.
The $6.5 billion raised in the Series H round isn’t even enough to cover half a year of data center rent. Therefore, Anthropic must forcefully sprint toward an IPO at a near-trillion-dollar valuation in October,
using the continuous flow of cheap capital from public markets to “ relay transfuse” these astronomical long-term leases.

Part 03 — The Why

Why Are They All Rushing to Go Public

This 2026 IPO wave is not the natural exit of these unicorns after achieving success,
but a “ transfusion race” forced by an unprecedentedly brutal capital burn. Behind every company lies an inescapable computing power black hole.

The essence of these IPOs is not “ cashing out after success,” but “ survival by amputation”

OpenAI: The Hundred-Billion Debt Cliff and Sora’s ROI Black Hole

OpenAI Crisis Transmission Chain

OpenAI’s predicament can be summarized as a triple overlay: revenue falling short of expectations, computing costs spiraling out of control, and long-term debt mounting.
In 2026, the company repeatedly missed monthly revenue targets, with growth stagnating as weekly active users plateaued at 900 million.
Meanwhile, moving from simple “ billion-scale text processing” to “ million-dimensional multimodal physical simulation”
(such as video generation and autonomous physical agents), computing expenditure has completely exceeded the carrying capacity of traditional VC.

Sora model’s ROI imbalance is merely the tip of the iceberg. The truly lethal factor is the $1.4 trillion in long-term data center and server hard leases — a legal obligation that must be fulfilled regardless of revenue performance. HSBC’s analysis indicates that even accounting for current revenue growth rates,
OpenAI still needs to supplement over $207 billion in pure cash flow before 2030 to maintain basic operations, otherwise it will face legal risks of computing power default.

SpaceX: The “ Financial Bomb” Brought by the xAI Merger

SpaceX Crisis Transmission Chain

SpaceX’s latest S-1 filing from May 2026, for the first time, revealed the severe financial damage caused by the xAI merger in Q1 2026.
To build the Colossus super data center and develop the Grok model, xAI burned through over $6 billion and $2.5 billion in computing expenditures in 2025 and Q1 2026, respectively.
This directly caused SpaceX to record a staggering $4.28 billion GAAP net loss in a single quarter in Q1 2026, with cumulative deficit reaching $41.3 billion by period end.

SpaceX must raise up to $40-80 billion in pure cash through the SPCX listing on NASDAQ to sustain this computing power black hole.
Otherwise, xAI’s continued hemorrhage will begin eroding its core Starlink business cash flow — and Starlink is currently SpaceX’s only business unit with positive cash flow.

Anthropic: $15 Billion/Year Rigid Liability Payment Pressure

Anthropic Crisis Transmission Chain

Although Anthropic has achieved great success in the enterprise market through “ Constitutional AI” and Project Glasswing, with Q2 revenue run rate projected to reach a remarkable $10.9 billion,
it is similarly locked into extremely expensive computing supply chains. Beyond the hundreds of billions in computing and chip contracts signed with Google, Broadcom, and Amazon,
Anthropic signed an exceptionally onerous contract with SpaceX in May 2026: leasing two orbital and ground data center clusters from SpaceX at $15 billion per year.

This means that even if Anthropic conducts no R&D whatsoever, it faces $15 billion in rigid payment obligations to SpaceX alone each year.
The $6.5 billion raised in the Series H round isn’t even enough to cover half a year of data center rent. Therefore, Anthropic must forcefully sprint toward an IPO at a near-trillion-dollar valuation in October,
using the continuous flow of cheap capital from public markets to “ relay transfuse” these astronomical long-term leases.

⚠ Hidden Financial Engineering: Cloud Credits Circular Financing

In this AI upstart IPO feast, a financial engineering scheme known as “ Cloud Credits Circular Financing” is systematically distorting supply and demand signals in capital markets.
Tech giants (Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Nvidia) inject funds into AI startups under the guise of “ investment,” but the funds are not cash — they are issued in the form of monopolistic cloud service credits (Cloud Credits).
These startups are forced to use the credits to purchase computing power from their investors, and the funds flow back to the giants’accounts in the form of “ server purchases/chip procurement,” and are recorded as the giants’ “ cloud business revenue”.
The same money is counted twice — once as investment, once as revenue — thereby manufacturing false demand signals and valuation bubbles.

Source: Blockonomi (May 2026); Tech Policy Press (Apr 14, 2026); Morningstar (May 6, 2026); Built In (Apr 21, 2026)

Circular Financing Case Investor Investment Amount Recirculation Commitment Recirculation Mechanism
Microsoft →OpenAI Microsoft $13 Billion OpenAI Annual Cloud Spend $60B+ Azure Credits →OpenAI Purchases Azure Compute →Microsoft Records as Cloud Revenue
Amazon →Anthropic Amazon $13 Billion (Cumulative) AWS Compute Commitment $100B Investment-to-Spend Ratio 20:1, Funds Flow Back to AWS
Alphabet →Anthropic Google $40 Billion (Committed) Google Cloud 5GW Compute Google Cloud Provides Compute →Anthropic Spend Recorded as Google Cloud Revenue
Nvidia →CoreWeave Nvidia $2 Billion (Jan 2026 Additional) $6.3 Billion Chip Purchase Order Nvidia Invests →CoreWeave Purchases Nvidia Chips →Nvidia Records as Chip Revenue
Oracle →OpenAI Oracle — $300 Billion GPU/Cloud Deal OpenAI Raises Funds →Capital Flows to Oracle →Oracle Stock Surges

Paper Profits vs. Real Cash Flow: A Shocking Contrast

The destructive consequences of this circular financing are directly reflected in the giants’financial statements. Alphabet reported a record $62.6 billion in profit in Q1 2026, but $28.7 billion (nearly half) came from paper appreciation of its Anthropic equity stake — which it helped price — rather than actual revenue from search ads, YouTube, or cloud business.
In the same quarter, Amazon reported $30.3 billion in profit, of which $16.8 billion similarly came from unrealized valuation gains on Anthropic equity.
However, Amazon’s free cash flow plummeted 95% to just $1.2 billion — because the company spent $44.2 billion building physical data centers.

The broader systemic risk is this: OpenAI and Anthropic together account for more than half of the $2 trillion in future cloud backlog orders for Microsoft, Oracle, Google, and Amazon.
Microsoft’s $13 billion OpenAI investment is disbursed as Azure credits, and OpenAI’s annual cloud spend ($60 billion) is already more than double its own revenue ($25 billion);
of Oracle’s $553 billion pipeline, 54% depends on OpenAI as a single customer.
These “ paper profits” contrast starkly with plunging free cash flows, and analysts have already drawn parallels between this pattern and Global Crossing and Qwest Communications from the 2001 dot-com bubble — when the two companies swapped fiber capacity to manufacture fake sales, ultimately leading Qwest to write off $1.4 billion in revenue and Global Crossing to file for bankruptcy.

Source: HumAI Blog (May 2026); FourWeekMBA (Apr 21, 2026); Blockonomi (May 2026); TipRanks (May 2026); CoreWeave Official Press Release (Jan 26, 2026)

A tech giant pours billions into an AI startup as “ investment,” but the contract contains strict clauses forcing that startup to hand that exact same money right back to the tech giant to rent their computer servers.

— BullTheoryio, Twitter/X (May 23, 2026); Quoted from Blockonomi / Morningstar Analysis Report
Regulatory Attention: The U.S. Federal Trade Commission (FTC) documented the financial relationship patterns in these AI partnerships in its January 2025 report.
Senators Elizabeth Warren and Ron Wyden consider cloud-AI partnerships to be equivalent to “ functional mergers designed to circumvent antitrust scrutiny”.
Tech Policy Press refers to this dynamic as the “ Cloud Credit Circuit” and notes:
“ When a startup’s cloud resource consumption is pre-funded by the cloud provider itself, the resulting usage statistics are real on the balance sheet but do not reflect arm’s-length transactions.”

Source: FTC Report (Jan 2025); Tech Policy Press (Apr 14, 2026); Senators Elizabeth Warren/Ron Wyden Statements

Financial Health Deep Comparison

Financial Health Indicator SpaceX Anthropic OpenAI
Free Cash Flow Starlink Barely Covering Devoured by $15B/Year Lease Deeply Negative
Debt/Lease Burden Surged After xAI Merger Hundreds of Billions in Compute Contracts $1.4 Trillion Hard Leases
Path to Profitability Clarity Starlink Predictable B2B Revenue High Growth Extremely Unclear
IPO Urgency Extremely High (June) Extremely High (October) High (Can Defer to 2027)
Core Risk Point xAI Hemorrhage Drags Down Core Business Rigid Leases Cannot Be Covered Computing Power Default Legal Risk

The current IPO frenzy is essentially an enormous “ risk transfer exercise.” The paper wealth accumulated through inflated valuations during the private phase is being transferred, through systematic modifications of index rules, to ordinary public investors, retail investors, and pension funds.

— Michael Hartnett, Chief Investment Strategist, Bank of America

Part 04 — Macro Context

Why Now, The Three Macro Forces

The 2026 IPO boom is not an accidental event, but the inevitable result of three converging macro forces.
Understanding these underlying drivers is essential to seeing the true nature of this capital game.

Capital Output Ratio Alert

Severe Distortion of Capital Output Ratio

Think tanks and bearish analysts keep presenting an unsettling set of data to the market:

Capital Output Ratio

=

Annual Computing Infrastructure Spending

Annual Actual Generative AI Revenue

≈
$500 Billion

$12 Billion

≈
42:1

This staggering figure far exceeds the telecom fiber-laying imbalance ratio during the dot-com bubble era. 95% of enterprise AI projects have zero ROI, causing LPs (Limited Partners) in the private VC space to begin widespread panic and capital withdrawal. As private equity markets gradually dry up and valuations face down-round pressure, choosing to list quickly on public markets has become the only exit path for early VCs and Silicon Valley insiders to cash out.

Data Source: MIT NANDA Report “ The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025”(Jul 2025); Sequoia Capital Analysis (Jun 2025)

Macro Context Driving Chain

1. The AI Arms Race Enters the “ Computing Bottomless Pit” Phase

From text to multimodal (video, physical simulation), computing demand expands exponentially. The capital output ratio reaches 42:1, far exceeding the 10:1 during the dot-com bubble.

2. Private Market Capital Drying Up, Valuations Facing Down-Round Pressure

95% of enterprise AI projects have zero ROI, LPs panicking and withdrawing capital. VCs can no longer sustain trillion-dollar-burning AI labs, forced to seek public market exits.

3. Index Inclusion Rule Arbitrage: “ Guaranteed Absorption” by Passive Funds

Nasdaq and S&P 500 modified rules, shortening the inclusion timeline for mega-IPOs from 12 months to 15 trading days, removing profitability thresholds. Ensuring passive funds must absorb at high prices.

This is not the natural realization of a technological revolution, but a “ forced listing wave” driven by the triple forces of the computing arms race, private capital exhaustion, and index rule arbitrage.

— Michael Hartnett, Chief Investment Strategist, Bank of America

Part 05 — The Debate

How Wall Street Views This

This extremely high-density financing demand has triggered intense divergence among investment banks, academic think tanks, and authoritative media in the public markets.
Essentially, two opposing camps have formed: one warns this is the greatest asset bubble in history, while the other insists this is the genuine realization of productivity gains.

Institution/Analyst Camp Core View Key Data
BofA Michael Hartnett Bearish “ Super Bubble” warning, tech sector weight will breach 48% red line Concentration exceeds 1920s/1970s/1980s Japan/2000 TMT
Citigroup Bearish Liquidity crowding-out concerns Trillion-level capital absorption will create market funding gaps
Morgan Stanley Thomas Kamei Bullish Definitely not a repeat of 2000 bubble, supported by solid cash flows Enterprise software revenue directly tied to cloud capex
JPMorgan Research Bullish AI investment directly linked to actual enterprise revenue Core value will accrue to real-economy companies applying AI
Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis Neutral Data AI investment contributes 0.97 percentage points to GDP Accounts for 39% of overall 2.51% growth rate
MIT NANDA Report Bearish 95% of enterprise AI projects have zero ROI Capital output ratio 42:1, far exceeding dot-com bubble

⚠ Bearish Camp: The “ Super Bubble” Theory

BofA’s Michael Hartnett issued a stern warning: the concentrated listing of SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic
will push the tech sector’s total weight in the S&P 500 index past the 48% historical red line.

This concentration already exceeds several extreme bubble peaks in modern financial history: the 1920s “ Roaring Twenties,” the 1970s “ Nifty Fifty,”
the late 1980s Japanese stock market bubble, and the 2000 TMT dot-com bubble.

Core judgment: This is an enormously scaled “ risk transfer exercise” — paper wealth accumulated during the private phase is being transferred to ordinary public investors, retail investors, and pension funds.

Citigroup similarly expressed concerns about liquidity crowding-out effects, believing that such concentrated trillion-level capital absorption will create significant market funding gaps.

✅ Bullish Camp: The Productivity Realization Theory

Morgan Stanley Counterpoint Global’s Thomas Kamei notes:
The current AI wave is definitely not a repeat of the 2000 dot-com bubble. 2000s internet companies generally lacked actual revenue support,
whereas the 2026 AI Big Three possess extremely solid cash flows and high-stickiness enterprise customer bases.

JPMorgan’s thematic research shows: current AI investments are not empty speculative concepts,
but are directly tied to actual enterprise software revenue and cloud computing capital expenditure.

Core logic: In every major technological wave, core value not only flows back to technology suppliers,
but also accrues through improved operating leverage and productivity to real-economy companies that actively apply the technology.

Academic Think Tanks’ “ Productivity Paradox” Assessment

Data Sources: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis; NBER Working Paper 34836 (Feb 2026); MIT NANDA Report (Jul 2025); Economic Times (Feb 18, 2026)

Compared to the highly conflicted games played by investment banks, neutral academic think tanks and macro-financial regulatory institutions focus more on risk feedback at the real economy level.
Research from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis shows that AI investment contributes approximately 0.97 percentage points to GDP, accounting for 39% of the overall 2.51% growth rate.
This data itself shows that AI investment is indeed driving economic growth, but the question is — is this driving force sustainable?

The MIT NANDA report raises sharper questions: 95% of enterprise AI projects have zero ROI.
This means the vast majority of enterprises have not generated actual returns from their AI investments, and the capital output ratio imbalance of 42:1 cannot persist indefinitely.
When enterprises begin cutting AI budgets, AI labs’revenue growth will face cliff-like declines.

Historical Bubble Comparison: 2026 vs 2000 Dot-Com Bubble

Dimension 2000 Dot-Com Bubble 2026 AI Bubble
Core Narrative Internet will transform all business models AI will replace human intellectual labor
Capital Output Imbalance Fiber Laying:Revenue ≈10:1 Compute Spending:Revenue ≈42:1
IPO Characteristics “.com” companies listing en masse, most with no revenue Trillion-level giants listing in concentration, massive losses
Index Manipulation Active funds chasing tech stocks Passive indices forced absorption, retail investors have no choice
Infrastructure Fiber, data centers (physical assets) GPU clusters, compute centers (extremely rapid depreciation)
Post-Burst Legacy Amazon, Google rise; broadband proliferation AI leaders consolidate; compute infrastructure commoditizes

Part 06 — The Mechanism

Passive Absorption Mechanism: The Hidden Channel of Index Arbitrage

In this IPO boom, there is also a widely overlooked but critically important structural factor:
the modern passive index investing system provides an almost guaranteed liquidity channel for these AI giants.

According to internal details disclosed by the Financial Times, to prepare for the concentrated arrival of trillion-level IPOs from SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic,
Nasdaq (announced March 30, 2026) and S&P 500 (launched consultation April 30, 2026) successively modified their index inclusion criteria,
significantly shortening the observation period for mega unprofitable giants and removing the hard threshold of consecutive profitability.
This means companies like SpaceX (ticker SPCX) and future Anthropic will be forcibly included in key benchmark indices such as the S&P 100 and Nasdaq 100
after only 15 trading days of listing.

Since trillions of dollars in global retirement funds, pension funds, and index ETFs (such as SPY, QQQ) must unconditionally buy and hold index constituents — this mechanism effectively ensures that no matter how severe these AI giants’fundamental losses are,
an enormous amount of passive public funds will enter after listing to “ support the floor,” thus providing a guaranteed liquidity channel for early high-risk venture investors.

Rule Transformation: From “ Strict Screening” to “ Wide-Open Doors”

Index Rule Dimension Old Rules (Pre-2026) New Rules (Post-2026 Modification) Change Magnitude
S&P 500 Listing Time Requirement At Least 12 Months 6 Months (Mega IPO) Shortened 50%
S&P 500 Profitability Requirement Most Recent Quarter + Four Consecutive Quarters Profitable Profitability Requirement Removed Completely Eliminated
S&P 500 Free Float Requirement At Least 10% Free Float Threshold Removed Completely Eliminated
Nasdaq-100 Observation Period 180 Days 15 Trading Days Shortened 92%
Nasdaq-100 Inclusion Speed Quarterly Review Immediate Inclusion (Top 40 by Market Cap) Real-time
FTSE Russell Fast-Track Inclusion Must Meet Multi-Dimensional Criteria Market Cap Qualification Enables Fast Track Significantly Simplified

Data Source: Nasdaq Official Announcement (Mar 30, 2026), Ashurst Legal Analysis (Apr 16, 2026), Bloomberg Linea, Xueqiu, LongPort, StockWireX

Passive Fund Scale: The Forced Absorbers

Passive Fund Type Assets Under Management (AUM) Tracking Benchmark Estimated Passive Absorption Scale
S&P 500 Index Funds $5.7 Trillion S&P 500 $20-40 Billion
Nasdaq 100 ETFs $2.1 Trillion Nasdaq-100 $15-30 Billion
Global Pension Funds $50 Trillion+ Benchmark Index Tracking Obligations $50-100 Billion
401(k) Retirement Plans $7 Trillion+ Target Date Fund Auto-Rebalancing $5-10 Billion
Total Estimate — — $80-160 Billion

Step 1: Listing

Only 5% minimal free float required to meet listing requirements, no profitability record needed.

Step 2: Wait 15 Trading Days

Triggers new index inclusion criteria, automatically enters observation list.

Step 3: Mandatory Inclusion in Benchmark Indices

Automatically absorbed as constituent stocks in S&P 100, Nasdaq 100, and other major indices.

Step 4: Trillions in Passive Funds Forced to Absorb

Global pension funds, index ETFs, etc. must allocate by weight, forming unconditional buying support.

Wealth Transfer Mechanism: From Retail Investors & Pension Funds to Insiders

The Wealth Transfer Chain from Ordinary People to Billionaires

1. Private Phase: Insiders Accumulate at Low Prices

Founders, early VCs, employees accumulate large share positions at extremely low valuations (SpaceX was only $3.3 billion in 2019). Musk holds 42% equity at near-zero cost.

2. Choose Valuation Peak for IPO

Companies only go public when they believe they can achieve the highest valuation. SpaceX from $3.3B →$1.75T, valuation expanding 52-60x. OpenAI expanding 85-110x.

3. Index Providers Modify Rules for Rapid Inclusion

Nasdaq, S&P, FTSE Russell intensively modify rules, compressing inclusion timeline from 12 months to 5-15 days, removing profitability and free float requirements.

4. Passive Funds Forced to Absorb at High Prices

Global $12 trillion in index funds forced to buy by weight, creating guaranteed liquidity for insiders. Retail investors indirectly bear costs through 401(k), pension funds.

The current IPO frenzy is essentially an enormous “ risk transfer exercise.” The paper wealth accumulated through inflated valuations during the private phase is being transferred, through systematic modifications of index rules, to ordinary public investors, retail investors, and pension funds.

— Michael Hartnett, Chief Investment Strategist, Bank of America

Historical Data: IPOs Are Poor Investments

Study/Indicator Time Range Core Finding
Renaissance Capital IPO Index Long-term Tracking IPOs underperform the market by 20-30% on average over 3 years
Jay Ritter Academic Research 1980-2023 Median 5-year post-IPO returns 18% lower than comparable public companies
2021 IPO Cohort 2021-2024 Over 60% of IPO companies’stock prices fell below offering price, average decline 45%
Tiger Global Fund 2021-2023 Heavy IPO tech stock positions caused fund net value drawdown of 65%

Regulatory Vacuum: The Unguarded Casino

Index providers (S&P Dow Jones, Nasdaq, FTSE Russell) are private companies, and their index compilation rules are not directly regulated by the SEC.
This means they can modify rules at their own discretion without any regulatory approval or public hearing.

The 2026 rule modifications were carried out precisely in this regulatory vacuum. Nasdaq announced modifications on March 30, effective May 1;
S&P 500 launched consultation in late April, proposed to take effect in early June — the entire process had no substantive review from any regulatory body.

As Ashurst law firm’s analysis pointed out: “ The index providers’rule modification process is opaque, standards are not publicly disclosed,
yet they directly affect trillions of dollars in asset allocation. This is a massive regulatory loophole.”

(Source: Ashurst Legal Analysis, Apr 16, 2026)

Part 07 — Index Rule Changes

Index Rule Modifications: A Carefully Designed Wealth Transfer

In 2026, major U.S. index providers intensively modified IPO inclusion rules, ostensibly to “ better reflect the market,”
but in reality opening a high-speed channel from ordinary people’s wallets to billionaires’pockets for mega-IPOs.
This is not an improvement in market efficiency, but an unregulated structural arbitrage.

Data Source: Nasdaq Official Announcement (Mar 30, 2026), Ashurst Legal Analysis (Apr 16, 2026), Bloomberg Linea, Xueqiu, LongPort, StockWireX

Index Provider Rule Modification Comparison

Index Provider Modification Date Original Rule New Rule Applicable Targets
Nasdaq-100 Announced 2026.3.30**Effective 5.1 180-Day Observation Period 15 Trading Days Top 40 IPOs by Market Cap
S&P 500 Late April 2026 Consultation**Proposed Early June Effective 12 Months + Profitability Requirement + 10% Free Float 6 Months + Profitability Removed + Free Float Threshold Removed “ Mega IPOs” with Market Cap ≈$200B
FTSE Russell May 2026 Multi-Dimensional Criteria Review Market Cap Qualification Fast Track Large Tech IPOs

Nasdaq’s 5x Multiplier Rule: The “ Nuclear Weapon” for Low Free Float Stocks

Nasdaq introduced a highly controversial “5x multiplier rule” in its March 2026 rule modification:
for mega-IPOs with free float ratios below 10%, when calculating index weight, their market capitalization is calculated at 5 times the actual free float.

What does this mean, Suppose SpaceX goes public at a $1.75 trillion valuation but only releases 5% free float (approximately $87.5 billion),
under the 5x multiplier rule, its weight in the Nasdaq 100 index would be calculated based on $437.5 billion — which equals 5 times its actual free float and 25% of its total valuation.

The direct consequence of this rule is that passive funds are forced to allocate these low-free-float stocks at extremely high weights,
while early insiders gain enormous index weight premiums on a minimal free float base.
This is a brazen “ leveraging small to control large” arbitrage mechanism.

Potential Consequences of Unregulated Loss of Control

Index providers are private companies whose rule modifications are not directly regulated by the SEC. This regulatory vacuum could lead to the following consequences:

  • Rule Arbitrariness: Index providers can modify rules at any time based on specific clients’needs without explanation or hearing.

  • Conflicts of Interest: Index providers may receive benefits from IPO underwriters, thereby tending to modify rules to favor specific IPOs.

  • Systemic Risk: When trillions in passive funds are forced to allocate according to distorted rules, market pricing mechanisms will be severely distorted.

  • Retail Investor Harm: Ultimately bearing the costs are ordinary workers who indirectly invest in indices through 401(k) s and pension funds.

Part 08 — Impact

Potential Impact: Systemic Risk Transmission Pathways

The impact of this IPO boom extends far beyond the three companies themselves. If the bubble bursts, risk will transmit through multiple pathways throughout the entire financial system.
Understanding these transmission mechanisms is key to assessing true risk exposure.

Impact Layers: Four-Level Transmission from Markets to Society

🏦

Level 1: Capital Markets

NASDAQ volatility surges, tech concentration breaches 48% red line, passive index funds forced to rebalance

🏛

Level 2: Banking System

U.S. banks’ $450B AI credit commitments face default risk, accounting for 25% of Tier 1 capital, potentially triggering capital adequacy crises

🏭

Level 3: Real Economy

AI investment accounts for 39% of GDP growth, if bubble bursts it will directly drag down economic growth, S&P Global predicts 2.5 million jobs lost

→
👥

Level 4: Social Level

Pension shrinkage, retirement fund losses, retail investor wealth evaporation, potentially triggering trust crises and regulatory backlash against the AI industry

Risk Transmission Pathway: If Post-IPO Stock Prices Break Below Offering Price

Systemic Risk Transmission Chain

Three Scenario Projections

🟢 25% Soft Landing

Anthropic enterprise revenue continues to grow; SpaceX Starlink cash flow covers xAI losses; OpenAI successfully reduces costs.
Market gradually digests high valuations.

Tech stocks moderate correction 10-15%

🟡 50% Volatile Divergence

Three companies IPO successfully but subsequent performance diverges; Anthropic outperforms, OpenAI/SpaceX under pressure.
Passive funds forced to rebalance.

NASDAQ volatility rises, individual stock divergence intensifies

🔴 25% Hard Landing

Post-IPO stock prices break below offering; productivity paradox confirmed; bank AI credit bad debts outbreak.
Triggers systemic risk.

NASDAQ could repeat 2000-style -60%+ correction

✅ Supporting Factors

  • Anthropic “ Constitutional AI” technical approach’s strict compliance advantage and 80% enterprise focus

  • Project Glasswing’s deep binding to the sovereign cybersecurity market

  • Brand premium and enterprise credibility endorsement from legal victories

  • PBC structure effectively circumvents complex SEC audits for non-profit-to-profit conversion

  • Fundamental support from actual enterprise software revenue and cloud computing capital expenditure

⚠ Risk Warnings

  • Capital output ratio 42:1 extreme imbalance is unsustainable

  • False demand signals manufactured by Cloud Credits circular financing

  • Index rule modifications tailor-made exit channels for insiders

  • Institutionalized wealth transfer through passive funds forced to absorb at high prices

  • Systemic risk exposure from banks’ $450B AI credit commitments

Part 09 — Conclusion

Echoes of History: The Mirror Image of the AI Bubble and the Dot-Com Bubble

The AI IPO boom led by SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic in 2026 forms a striking historical mirror image with the dot-com bubble twenty-five years ago.
Both bubbles carried grand narratives of technological revolution, both experienced frenzied capital inflows, and both face the ultimate test of productivity realization.
However, the true value of history lies not in simple analogy, but in understanding the subtle and dangerous dividing line between technological revolution and capital bubbles.

Structural Comparison of the Two Bubbles

Dimension 2000 Dot-Com Bubble 2026 AI Bubble
Core Narrative Internet will transform all business models AI will replace human intellectual labor
Capital Output Imbalance Fiber Laying:Revenue ≈10:1 Compute Spending:Revenue ≈42:1
IPO Characteristics “.com” companies listing en masse, most with no revenue Trillion-level giants listing in concentration, massive losses
Index Manipulation Active funds chasing tech stocks Passive indices forced absorption, retail investors have no choice
Infrastructure Fiber, data centers (physical assets) GPU clusters, compute centers (extremely rapid depreciation)
Post-Burst Legacy Amazon, Google rise; broadband proliferation AI leaders consolidate; compute infrastructure commoditizes

Productivity Leap: The True Gift of Technological Revolution

History tells us that the bursting of technology bubbles often leaves genuine legacies. After the 2000 dot-com bubble burst,
the overbuilding of fiber infrastructure laid the foundation for subsequent broadband proliferation, and Amazon and Google rose from the ruins to become trillion-dollar giants.

The AI technology revolution similarly possesses genuine potential. Data from the Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis shows that AI investment has contributed 39% of GDP growth.
In areas such as medical diagnosis, drug discovery, code generation, and scientific computing, AI is indeed producing quantifiable efficiency improvements.

The question is —are these genuine productivity improvements sufficient to sustain the current $3 trillion valuation?
The capital output ratio imbalance of 42:1 means that behind every dollar of AI revenue, there is $42 in computing investment.
This imbalance cannot persist indefinitely.

The Heavy Lesson of 2000: Bursting Is Not a Gentle Correction

The NASDAQ Composite Index reached its peak of 5,048 in March 2000, then experienced a 31-month decline,
hitting bottom at 1,114 in October 2002 —a decline of 78%.
This means investors who bought at the peak lost more than three-quarters of their wealth.

An even more far-reaching impact was the innovation winter. From 2000 to 2005, Silicon Valley venture capital plummeted 80%,
countless startups folded, and tech talent dispersed. It wasn’t until the iPhone launch in 2007 that the tech industry truly emerged from the trough.

If the 2026 AI bubble follows a similar script, the consequences could be even more severe — because this bubble’s participants include not only risk-tolerant active investors,
but also hundreds of millions of ordinary workers who are forced to participate through passive index funds.

The Most Dangerous Harvest: “ Guaranteed Absorption” Under the Passive Index Mechanism

In this bubble, the most outrageous aspect is not the inflated valuations, but the institutionalized wealth transfer mechanism.
Nasdaq and S&P 500 successively modified index inclusion rules in 2026, shortening the observation period for mega unprofitable giants from years to 15 trading days,
and removing the consecutive profitability threshold. This is not a neutral “ market rule adjustment,” but an exit channel tailor-made for specific interest groups.

When SpaceX and Anthropic are forcibly included in key benchmark indices such as the S&P 100 and Nasdaq 100 just 15 days after listing,
trillions of dollars in global retirement funds and index ETFs must unconditionally buy — regardless of whether their valuations are reasonable,
whether their business models are sustainable, whether retail investors wish to participate. The essence of this “ passive absorption” mechanism is
to transfer the exit risk of early VCs, founders, and insiders through the “ mechanical nature” of index investing,
forcibly imposing it on every ordinary worker contributing to a pension fund.

This is the most sophisticated and most ruthless wealth extraction in modern financial history: equity acquired by early capital at pennies
is sold to the public market at valuations of hundreds of dollars per share at IPO; and when stock prices ultimately revert to fundamentals,
those bearing the losses are the index fund holders who never actively chose to invest in these companies — teachers, nurses, firefighters, factory workers’retirement savings. These capitalists who harvest retail investors by modifying rules and exploiting “ guaranteed absorption” by passive funds
are not value-creating investors, but wealth-transferring predators.

Wealth Transfer Alert

Index Inclusion Rule Modifications = Institutionalized Retail Investor Harvesting

When rule-makers and vested interests collude, the “ passivity” of passive investing becomes the perfect harvesting tool:

Early VCs Buy at
$0.01/Share
→IPO Priced at
$300/Share
→Index Funds Forced to Absorb →Retail Pension Funds Bear Downside Risk

Privatized Profits, Socialized Losses — This Is the Most Naked Truth of the 2026 AI IPO Boom

Final Judgment: Between Hope and Warning

The genuine potential of the AI technology revolution is undeniable, but technological revolution and capital bubbles are never synonymous.
The legacy of the dot-com bubble was Amazon and Google, but its cost was the wealth destruction of a generation of investors and a five-year innovation winter.
If the 2026 AI bubble bursts, its legacy may be computing infrastructure and breakthroughs in certain vertical domains,
but its cost may be systemic shock to the global financial system and pension shrinkage for tens of millions of ordinary workers.

Even more dangerous, this bubble’s harvesting mechanism is far more concealed and institutionalized than that of 2000.
When passive index funds become tools for “ guaranteed absorption,” when index rule modifications become channels for benefit transfers,
the “ price discovery” function of market economies has been severely distorted. This is not a failure of free markets, but the conscious manipulation of rules.

Core Judgment Framework

Key Variables

**History does not repeat itself, but it rhymes. The 2000 dot-com bubble taught us: technological revolutions will ultimately be realized, but the vast majority of bubble participants will become fuel rather than passengers. If the 2026 AI bubble follows the same script, the cruelest difference is —this time, retail investors don’t even have the right to choose not to participate, their pension funds have been tied to this gamble by the modifiers of index rules. For professional investors and regulators, while pursuing the trillion-dollar tech mythology, they must maintain the highest level of vigilance against the predatory nature of the passive absorption mechanism, the financial black hole of high computing depreciation, and the systemic risk of bank credit. Technology is worth believing in, but capitalists’greed is always worth questioning.
Primary Data Sources:
Company Financial Data: SpaceX S-1 Filing (SEC EDGAR, May 20, 2026), Morningstar, NDTV Profit, Securities Times, PitchBook
Valuation & IPO Information: Bloomberg (Apr-May 2026), Reuters, CNBC, TechStackIPO.com, The Information, TechJournal.org
Anthropic Data: Anthropic Official Announcement (May 28, 2026), 36Kr (Jun 2, 2026), Toutiao, Shepherd Gazette
OpenAI Data: The AI Journal (May 2026), RoboRhythms (May 22, 2026), The Planet Tools, AI2.work
IPO Market Data: KPMG “ China Mainland and Hong Kong IPO Market Q1 2026 Review”(Apr 2026), IPOX SchusterWatch Q1 2026, Dealogic ECM Highlights 1Q26, EY-Parthenon
AI Investment & Productivity: MIT NANDA “ The GenAI Divide: State of AI in Business 2025”(Jul 2025), NBER Working Paper 34836 (Feb 2026), Goldman Sachs Research (Mar 2026), Futurum Group (Feb 2026), Sequoia Capital Analysis
Index Rule Modifications: Nasdaq Official Announcement (Mar 30, 2026), Ashurst Legal Analysis (Apr 16, 2026), Bloomberg Linea, Xueqiu, LongPort, StockWireX
Market Analysis: BofA Michael Hartnett, Morgan Stanley Counterpoint Global, JPMorgan Research, HSBC Analyst Reports, S&P Global Research
Report Date: June 2026 | Version: v2.1 (Data Source Verified Edition)

© 2026 IPO Deep Dive Report. All data verified and sourced from public SEC filings, Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, KPMG, MIT NANDA Report, NBER Working Papers, and other authoritative financial research institutions.

Report Version: v2.1 | Data Verification Date: June 2026 | All figures cross-referenced with primary sources where available

entry.title: Visiting a Catholic Church in Chengdu
entry.content:
Yesterday I went on a church retreat[^retreat] (non-parishioners aren’t allowed in) — couldn’t make it to an English Mass
So I woke up very early this morning to attend the earliest Mass
And on this day of Lent, I observed both the fasting and abstinence — no breakfast yet (the word “breakfast”literally means breaking the fast)
Now I am the person who best fits this Jubilee Year’s[^jubilee] theme: “Pilgrims of Hope”
But but actually the biggest reason I came here is that this is the cathedral of Chengdu — the only place in all of Sichuan-Chongqing[^sichuan-chongqing] that has Luce[^luce], possibly the entire Southwest
I thought I’d have to pay for them, but the church staff just handed me a whole handful, for free
Bliss! Happy lucky smile yeah!️
Luce is so cute, the Cathedral of the Immaculate Conception in Chengdu is so kind meow[^meow], wishing the progressive Pope Francis eternal health and triumph
Amen

💬 Comments

  • optional (2025-03-23 10:09): You’ve been thoroughly marinated
  • LoulanRE reply @optional (2025-03-23 10:34): They said lots of Chinese I’d never heard before, imitating how parishioners talk is really fun, you could say it’s a kind of cosplay

[^retreat]: A retreat in Catholic context is a period of spiritual withdrawal for prayer and reflection, typically held at a church or monastery.
[^jubilee]: A Jubilee Year in Catholic tradition is a special year of remission of sins and universal pardon, typically celebrated every 25 years. The 2025 Jubilee’s theme is “Pilgrims of Hope.”
[^sichuan-chongqing]: Sichuan-Chongqing is a common Chinese term referring collectively to Sichuan Province and Chongqing Municipality — two regions that share deep cultural and historical ties, having been a single province until 1997.
[^luce]: Luce is the official mascot of the 2025 Catholic Jubilee Year, designed by the Vatican — a cartoon-style pilgrim girl with blue hair and a cross-shaped walking stick. She became wildly popular, especially among Asian Catholic communities.
[^meow]: “Meow” is a Chinese internet slang particle appended to sentences for a cute, playful tone — borrowed from Japanese internet culture’s “にゃ”(nya).

entry.title: Code Display & File Download Demo
entry.content:
The Complete Guide to Code Display & File Downloads

This article demonstrates how to elegantly display code in your blog and provide file download functionality.

Tech Stack & Plugins

Layer Tech/Plugin Description
Blog Framework Hexo v8.1.1 Static blog generator
Theme Butterfly v5.5.4 Provides code highlighting, Mac-style code blocks, copy button
Code Highlighting PrismJS Theme darker, supports auto-wrap & height limit
Custom Tag download-tag.js Custom Hexo Tag plugin, provides codefile & filecard tags
Style Injection darkmode.css Custom download button & card styles, supports light/dark theme switching

1. Basic Code Block (Built-in Copy Button)

The Butterfly theme already has built-in code copy functionality. Just use standard Markdown syntax:

Hover over the top-right corner of the code block, and a copy button will appear, click it to copy the entire code.

2. Code Block with Download Button

If you want readers to directly download a .py file, use the custom download tag:

fibonacci.py

Download File

Syntax Reference:

Click the Download File button above, and the browser will directly download the fibonacci.py file.

3. File Download Card

If you want to provide a download link for an external file, use the filecard tag:

Data Analysis Script.py Python 12.5 KB Download

Syntax Reference:

This card automatically displays the corresponding icon based on the file extension (e.g., for Python).

4. More Code Highlighting Examples

JavaScript

utils.js

Download File

TypeScript

api-client.ts

Download File

Rust

main.rs

Download File

5. Download Cards for Various File Types

Project Source Code.zip ZIP 2.3 MB Download

Design File.fig Figma 15.8 MB Download

Paper.pdf PDF 856 KB Download

6. Usage Recommendations

Scenario Recommended Approach Reason
Short code snippets Regular code block Simple, built-in copy function
Complete runnable scripts codefile tag Readers can download and run directly
External resources / large files filecard tag Aesthetic, shows file info
Multi-file packages filecard + ZIP Convenient batch download

7. Configuration Notes

The following configurations have been set up for you:

  • Code highlighting theme: Switched to darker dark theme, better suited for technical content
  • Mac-style code block: Enabled rounded corners and title bar style
  • Code auto-wrap: Avoids horizontal scrollbar
  • Code height limit: Shows expand button when exceeding 500px
  • Download button styles: Injected into darkmode.css, supports light/dark theme switching

No additional setup needed, just use the syntax above in your posts!

entry.title: AI Parallel Development with OpenCode in JupyterHub Terminal
entry.content:
AI Parallel Development with OpenCode in JupyterHub Terminal

JupyterHub’s Terminal is a seriously underrated feature. Most people only use JupyterHub for Notebooks, unaware that behind that inconspicuous Terminal icon lies a full Linux terminal, one powerful enough to support an AI-assisted parallel development workflow. This article introduces how to use JupyterHub Terminal + tmux + OpenCode to set up a lightweight yet complete AI parallel development environment right in your browser. If your server resources are already tight, this setup adds virtually zero overhead.

Why JupyterHub Terminal

JupyterHub’s Terminal: The Overlooked Gateway

JupyterHub is ubiquitous in academic and team settings, shared lab servers, course platforms, enterprise data analysis environments, you name it. But the vast majority of users stick to the Notebook interface: writing Python, running experiments, viewing charts.

That Terminal button in the Launcher, Rarely clicked.

That’s a shame. JupyterHub’s Terminal is essentially a full Bash terminal running directly on the server. It requires no extra installation, no SSH key configuration, no local client setup, just open your browser and go. For environments already running JupyterHub, it’s a zero-cost Linux workspace entry point.

Resource Scarcity: Why This Matters

On shared servers or low-spec cloud instances (2C4G or even less), every megabyte of RAM counts. VS Code Server gobbles up 1G+ just to start, and remote desktop solutions are even worse. The Terminal, being a pure text interface, adds virtually no extra resource consumption, it’s already running on the server with no additional rendering overhead.

This means you don’t have to squeeze your already-tight memory just to get a “decent development environment.”The Terminal leaves all the saved resources for your actual tasks.

The Core Value of This Setup

Using JupyterHub Terminal as your entry point, paired with tmux (session persistence) and OpenCode (AI coding assistant), you get:

  • Browser as development environment: No local tool installation needed, open a webpage and start working
  • Uninterrupted sessions: tmux decouples terminal sessions from the browser connection. Close the browser, lose network, switch devices, your tasks keep running
  • AI parallel development: Two tmux sessions each running an OpenCode instance, one exploring, one implementing, progressing in parallel
  • Zero extra overhead: Terminal + tmux memory usage is negligible, leaving all resources for actual tasks

Overall Approach

  • Environment: Ubuntu cloud server (with JupyterHub deployed) + Terminal + tmux + OpenCode
  • Task splitting: One Terminal handles “exploration”(e.g., feature engineering ideas), another handles “implementation”(e.g., feature code), running in parallel
  • Background protection: Each task runs in an independent tmux session. Closing the browser or losing network won’t kill the task, it continues executing in the server background

If you run tasks directly in the Terminal without tmux, closing the tab or experiencing network fluctuation will most likely terminate them. Except for very brief tests, using tmux throughout is the safe approach.

Workflow Architecture

1. Prerequisites (One-time Setup Only)

1.1 Enable 8 GB Swap (Requires sudo)

For a server with 4GB RAM, Swap isn’t optional, it’s a necessity. OpenCode consumes memory at runtime, and combined with Python data processing, physical memory runs out quickly. Without Swap, the OOM Killer will terminate your processes without mercy.

After completion, run free -h and confirm the Swap line shows 8.0Gi. If your account lacks sudo access, forward these commands to your server administrator.

1.2 Install tmux

1.3 Install Node.js 20+ and OpenCode

OpenCode requires Node.js, 20. If the server already has a suitable version, skip the Node.js installation.

Check the current version:

If the version is below 20, install via nvm (avoids conflicts with existing system Node):

Then install OpenCode (globally):

Close the current Terminal and reopen a new one, then run opencode --version to verify.

2. Upload Local Skills Folder and Overwrite OpenCode Global Path

OpenCode reads knowledge bases from ~/.config/opencode/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md. This step uploads your local skills folder to the server and uses cp -r to fully overwrite the corresponding skill directory.

2.1 Upload the Folder via JupyterHub

In JupyterHub’s file management interface (the URL typically ends with /tree), drag and drop your local skills folder onto the file list area to upload. Once uploaded, the folder will appear in your home directory on the server (e.g., /home/your-username/skills).

2.2 Overwrite to OpenCode Skills Directory

Assuming the skill you want to use is named feature-engineering, run the following commands to copy the uploaded skills folder contents to the target location:

Verify the core file is in place:

If it outputs your skills content normally, the deployment is complete. When you start OpenCode next time, it will automatically recognize and load the knowledge from that directory.

3. Running Two Tasks in Parallel

3.1 Open Two Terminal Tabs

In JupyterHub’s Launcher, click Terminal twice to get two side-by-side terminal tabs.

3.2 tmux Session Lifecycle

3.3 Left Terminal: Exploration Task

3.4 Right Terminal: Implementation Task

Detach but keep tasks running: Press Ctrl+b, then press d.
Reattach to check: In any Terminal, run tmux attach -t explore or tmux attach -t implement.

For specific task descriptions and interaction methods, complete them within the OpenCode interface. For detailed usage, check the OpenCode Official Documentation.

4. Resource Monitoring (Third Terminal, Recommended)

Open a third Terminal tab and use btop to monitor memory and Swap usage in real time:

Press q to exit monitoring.

In resource-constrained environments, developing a monitoring habit is especially important. When you notice Swap utilization climbing steadily, it means physical RAM is running low, at that point, consider reducing parallelism and letting one task finish before starting the next.

5. FAQ

Symptom Possible Cause Solution
opencode shows command not found Installation incomplete or PATH not refreshed Reopen Terminal and try again
tmux attach cannot enter session Session lost (e.g., server rebooted) Run tmux ls to check; if the list is empty, recreate the session
Tasks run slowly or get interrupted Memory pressure Confirm Swap is enabled in btop; if Swap is also exhausted, consider reducing parallelism
Uploaded folder name is not skills Original name was preserved during upload Replace ~/skills in subsequent commands with the actual folder name
JupyterHub Terminal won’t open Browser blocked popup Allow popups for the site, or manually open in a new tab

6. Additional Notes

  • Two tasks should use different output paths or filenames to avoid overwriting each other.
  • The Swap file doesn’t need to be deleted, keep it for future use.
  • Even if you close the browser, as long as tmux sessions exist, tasks won’t be interrupted.
  • If the server reboots, tmux sessions will be lost and need to be recreated. However, OpenCode’s skills configuration won’t be lost.

7. Why This Works: The Value of This Approach

Back to the opening question: why do this in JupyterHub Terminal?

Terminal is JupyterHub’s hidden superpower. It requires no extra deployment, no admin approval, no client installation, as long as you have a JupyterHub account, you have a full Linux terminal. It’s a zero-barrier starting point.

tmux decouples terminal sessions from the browser. JupyterHub’s Terminal relies on WebSocket connections, once the network drops, the terminal freezes. But tmux keeps processes in the server background, completely decoupled from the frontend connection. You can check progress from your phone via JupyterHub, or close your laptop to grab lunch, come back, tmux attach, and everything is right where you left off.

OpenCode upgrades Terminal from “command line”to “AI workstation.” Pure terminal interaction isn’t exactly beginner-friendly, but OpenCode’s TUI interface lowers the interaction complexity. More importantly, through the skills mechanism, you can inject domain knowledge into the AI and have it work according to your specifications and thought processes, this isn’t a generic chatbot, but a dedicated assistant with a “domain brain.”

Parallelism is an efficiency multiplier. Exploration and implementation are two different cognitive modes: exploration demands divergent thinking, implementation demands focused execution. Switching between them serially wastes enormous time on context switching. Two tmux sessions each running an OpenCode, one trying things out while the other lands code, nearly doubles overall efficiency. In resource-constrained environments, this time savings is especially precious.

Zero resource waste. Terminal + tmux memory usage is negligible, and OpenCode itself is lightweight. Compared to VS Code Server’s typical 1G+ overhead, this setup leaves every megabyte of RAM for your actual tasks. On a 4GB machine, this could be the difference between “runs fine”and “can’t run at all.”

This approach applies far beyond feature engineering. Hyperparameter tuning, data cleaning, code refactoring, documentation writing, any scenario that can be split into “exploration + implementation”or “Task A + Task B”can reuse the same workflow. If you already have JupyterHub, give that Terminal button a click, it’s more powerful than you think.

entry.title: Watching the Typhoon
entry.content:
Went to see the typhoon

The promise was made almost out of defiance, after hearing He[^1] talk about hurricane-watching activities in America

During a previous group outing, we ran all the way to a flower field near the Jimei Bridge[^2], and decided this place that reminds one of Sea and Bouquet[^3] would be the spot

In truth, I didn’t experience the typhoon’s full might, just an ordinary rainstorm

My state of mind had shifted too. Life is a vast sea[^4]; regret for the past leaves only silence. Can the emotions with nowhere to go truly recede with the tide?

Walking back to school from Fenglin along the coastline, six years of the commute route, I’m familiar with nearly every building. But I probably won’t come back again, and I won’t be able to walk into the school anymore either

This time we started from where we left off last time, near an observation deck, the one you have to specially climb over a railing to get into

Two months later, I return here. Strong winds blowing, standing on the side where the dust has settled, gazing out at the gray distance

A small boat departs from here onward; I entrust the rest of my life to the river and sea[^5]

<video controls style=”max-width:100%;”preload=”metadata”><source src=”/img/qzone/2024-07-25_v3.mp4”type=”video/mp4”>Your browser does not support video playback

<video controls style=”max-width:100%;”preload=”metadata”><source src=”/img/qzone/2024-07-25_v4.mp4”type=”video/mp4”>Your browser does not support video playback

<video controls style=”max-width:100%;”preload=”metadata”><source src=”/img/qzone/2024-07-25_v5.mp4”type=”video/mp4”>Your browser does not support video playback

<video controls style=”max-width:100%;”preload=”metadata”><source src=”/img/qzone/2024-07-25_v6.mp4”type=”video/mp4”>Your browser does not support video playback

Footnotes

[^1]: He, a friend of the author
[^2]: Jimei Bridge, a cross-sea bridge in Xiamen
[^3]: Sea and Bouquet, a Japanese song
[^4]: “Life is a vast sea”, a Minnan (Southern Fujian) dialect expression meaning “life is like the vast and undulating sea”
[^5]: “A small boat departs from here onward; I entrust the rest of my life to the river and sea”(, a celebrated line from the Song dynasty poet Su Shi’s Lin Jiang Xian (. The full sentiment conveys a yearning to leave the worldly life behind and drift freely upon the waters.

entry.title: Daily Rituals How Artists Work - Introduction
entry.content:
Daily Rituals How Artists Work (Mason Currey)

How It Started

I came across this book while doing further reading from an article translated by Jarrett Ye, Cultivating Depth and Stillness in Research - Zhihu [^zhihu]

The mistaken expectation is believing that progress should always be swift and constantly rewarding, and then spiraling into fear and self-doubt when you feel you’re not at your best

What This Book Is About

Epigraph: “Who could reveal the essence and mark of the artistic temperament, Who could comprehend that profound and instinctive union, the balance struck between discipline and abandon!”?Thomas Mann, Death in Venice

As mentioned above, this book collects the daily schedules and specific activities of many remarkable figures, such as

Karl Marx, Author of Das Kapital, founding thinker of modern communist thought

Friedrich Nietzsche, Known for the philosophical work Thus Spoke Zarathustra

Ludwig van Beethoven, Hailed as the “Saint of Music,”his Symphony No. 9 is widely known

Albert Einstein, Not only a scientist, but also known for his distinctive way of life

Ernest Hemingway, Author of The Old Man and the Sea, Nobel Prize in Literature laureate

Franz Kafka, Renowned for The Metamorphosis and absurd literature

This helps with reflecting on one’s own time management. Their extraordinary achievements lend a certain authority to their methods, and besides, reading these anecdotes in your spare time is genuinely entertaining

Nearly every weekday morning for a year and a half, I got up at 5:30, brushed my teeth, made a cup of coffee, and sat down to write about how some of the greatest minds of the past four hundred years approached this exact same taskhat is, how they made the time each day to do their best work, how they organized their schedules in order to be creative and productive. By writing about the admittedly mundane details of my subjects’ daily liveshen they slept and ate and worked and worried hoped to provide a novel angle on their personalities and careers, to sketch entertaining, small-bore portraits of the artist as a creature of habit.

Why This Book

My first semester of college was wrapping up, and while I enjoyed more freedom, I also desperately felt the need for self-management skills. I tried looking for methods to learn from, but the information entropy on the internet is absurdly low, the same trite techniques everywhere, yet it’s hard to know if anyone has actually followed through or what the results were. Instead, all you find are course-selling pitches. While continuing to experiment with those methods, I also hoped that the cases from predecessors in this book could broaden my thinking

But it’s important to note that these aren’t meant to be simply copied. We need to account for the difference in eras, past practices may have limitations when applied today, especially now that we live in a digital information society with far more efficient tools and methods available. That doesn’t mean reading about these things is less valuable, though. When people share their own method summaries, they often say, “There are countless methods out there, choosing what suits you is what matters best.”I want to add something for many people at the same time: “Although methods can’t be directly copied, the principles are universally applicable.” I hope that when you read, you can also distill the reasons behind the success of these practices and build your own models, that’s the approach that truly works

Also, I wanted to read and accumulate from the original English text, being able to connect learning with material I’m genuinely interested in. I personally find that learning anecdotes is a kind of refined pleasure; the thought of being able to tell celebrity stories to others in the future makes me want to collect them all. Not only that, but the original prose is brilliant, it rewards multiple readings

After reading a bit more, I felt an even stronger need to recommend it to everyone, because it practically nailed my own situation

In reality, most of the people in this book are somewhere in the middleommitted to daily work but never entirely confident of their progress; always wary of the one-off day that undoes the streak.

When I made up my mind to rearrange and plan my study time, I pulled this together from my Obsidian journal vault, by mid-December I hadn’t written a single entry. Every day was just a daily disaster log

That was November 30th, I was helping a classmate with a question but copied it wrong, and when I couldn’t solve it, I wrote a big “GG”on the page. Little did I know that was prophetic, shortly after, I caught the flu, slept through three groggy days, and my rhythm was completely shattered

After that, I forgot what I was supposed to do each day. My biological clock was a mess, my sleep was poor, and I couldn’t focus on anything. Anxiety, staying up late, giving up, a perfect negative feedback loop, until December 19th when I finally started re-planning and using my time again

(Of course, the fact that any disruption can derail your work rhythm is indeed a problem, after reflection, I’ve started trying to use daily and weekly checklists)

Sharing

I trained ChatGPT to translate the original text in a style that is (primarily reflective in tone, logically clear, attentive to layers and detail, while quoting famous sayings or classical theories to add depth to the expression), and I maintained the format of [potentially unfamiliar vocabulary (beyond the 3,500-word gaokao[^gaokao] range)], [concept introductions (beyond common university student knowledge)], and [translation (paragraph-by-paragraph bilingual comparison)], this not only enables vocabulary accumulation but also broadens cultural horizons and promotes English reading and writing

An example from proofreading:

In that sense, this is a superficial book. It is about the circumstances of creative activity, not the product; it deals with manufacturing rather than meaning.

ChatGPT translated “superficial“as [ㄩ, surface-level], but I think “ゆ/shallow”is a better translation

ChatGPT: Using the translation “ㄩ?, In that sense, this is a “ㄩ, book. It concerns itself with the circumstantial conditions of creative activity, not the work itself; it focuses on the manufacturing process of creation, not its meaning.
Pros:
Neutral and accurate: Compared to “ゆ,””ㄩ, is more neutral and better expresses the author’s intent of stating a fact rather than making a critique.
Fits the context: The following text mentions the book focuses on “circumstantial conditions”rather than “meaning”?”ㄩ, better reflects this objective description

Me: But “ㄩ, is rarely used and hard to understand. Plus, the author calling their own book “ゆ”?that kind of self-deprecating humor is actually quite charming

ChatGPT: You make an excellent point! In the Chinese context, “ゆ”is indeed more common and easier to understand, and it better conveys the author’s self-deprecating humor and lighthearted tone. Using “ゆ”not only aligns with the original semantics but also adds playfulness and makes it more relatable to readers

Me: I want you to keep optimizing your translations the way we discussed “superficial”being better translated as “ゆ”

ChatGPT: Understood! I’ll continue refining the translations, paying attention to details and natural expression to better meet your expectations. Feel free to let me know if you have other preferences or needs

Of course, this is just my own taste. After all, there’s a line in the original that goes:

John Cheever thought that you couldn’t even type a business letter without revealing something of your inner selfsn’t that the truth?

If you happen to like this style too, that’s the best

Here’s to wriggling through.

That’s all

[^zhihu]: Zhihu (ヤ) is China’s largest Q&A platform, often called “the Chinese Quora”?but with longer, more essay-style answers and a stronger culture of in-depth knowledge sharing.
[^gaokao]: The gaokao ( is China’s national college entrance examination. The “3,500-word range”refers to the official vocabulary list for the English section of the gaokao, essentially the baseline English vocabulary expected of Chinese high school graduates.

entry.title: Fandom Victory
entry.content:
It reached him, it reached him![^1]
Read so many of his personal stories and translated articles
Inspired by that open-source spirit, I made some contributions of my own
My further reading on the original text got noticed
FAN!DOM! VIC!TORY!
In an instant, understood, became, a cognition stan[^2]
Haha, alright, today I’m the happiest

[^1]: “, (todoketa), Chinese internet slang derived from Japanese, (todokita, “it reached/it got through”). It means “my message reached him”or “he saw it,”often used when a fan’s work or message gets noticed by someone they admire. Here, the author is thrilled that their contribution was seen by an open-source knowledge sharer they look up to.
[^2]: “ょ, (rnzh ch), internet slang combining “ょ”(cognition/knowing) with “, (from Japanese, meaning obsessive fan). It refers to someone who is deeply obsessed with understanding and knowing everything about a particular person or subject. A “cognition stan”?someone whose fandom manifests through the desire to deeply know and understand.

entry.title: Dark-and-Darker Three-Day Binge
entry.content:
Three days of playtime over the weekend
So fun it felt like being young again
The gameplay is an adventurer party exploring dungeons, JRPG-style Western fantasy, D&D
If you love Frieren[^1] or Delicious in Dungeon[^2], you absolutely have to try it
Currently short on backline/support, Sorcerers/Wizards/Warlocks/Druids/Bards/Rangers etc. needed
Fighters/Barbarians/Rogues equally welcome
Though my class is Cleric, my cantrips and gear setup have me fully frontline tanking
Free to play on Steam
Come explore dungeons in Dark and Darker



** Comments**

  • abc (2025-05-26 00:26): We need more people

[^1]: Frieren: Beyond Journey’s End (, a Japanese manga and anime series following an elf mage’s journey after the defeat of the Demon King, acclaimed for its contemplative take on mortality and memory.
[^2]: Delicious in Dungeon (с?, a Japanese manga and anime series where an adventuring party cooks and eats the monsters they encounter in a dungeon. A beloved blend of fantasy and gourmet cooking.

entry.title: Fever Dream of Napoleon
entry.content:
Last night I had a fever and it was like being possessed[^possessed]

Maybe because I was reading Les Misrables before bed, and then I dreamed about the French Revolution (but Les Misrables isn’t about the French Revolution

Because we couldn’t stand the heavy taxes, we all chopped off Louis together

Then followed Napoleon from Toulon, the Alps, Egypt all the way to the Brumaire

I had barely reached the Arc de Triomphe when I woke up, and then in a very muddled state I was in bed trying desperately to cheer for Napoleon’s coronation, tried for over ten minutes, got a bit thirsty and wanted to drink water, and while sitting in the chair drinking water I started thinking about what on earth I was just doing

Went back to bed but couldn’t sleep either, kept thinking about the anti-French alliances, my own thoughts were almost drowned out, nothing but moving artillery and cavalry flanks, until I finally realized I could still speak, could act on my own thoughts, I was still myself, only then could I fall asleep in peace

** Comments**

  • Sanjiaomao (2022-12-22 10:58): Get some good rest

[^possessed]: “Possessed”, literally “seized and replaced”?is a Chinese internet slang term borrowed from Taoist mythology, meaning your body has been taken over by another entity. In casual use, it describes feeling like you’re acting completely out of character, as if someone else is piloting your body.

entry.title: Gasoline on the Tour Bus
entry.content: Gasoline on the tour bus, thinking itself a passenger too.

entry.title: Green Train to Chengdu at Dawn
entry.content:
Green-skin train[^1] at dawn, clackety-clack[^2], crashing into Chengdu

** Comments**

  • LoulanRE (2025-03-21 07:52): Just stopped at Suining station. The government buildings on the railway side planted vast fields of rapeseed flowers. Looking off into the distance, there’s a residential street with buildings embraced by courtyards of flower seas. All along the way, wherever there are people, there are rolling fields of rapeseed blossoms following the terrain. You’d expect rice seedlings only after Qingming, and rice isn’t the main crop now, but who’d have thought they’d sow fields of gold everywhere, often dotted with white-flowered plum trees. You can imagine, they are romantics, and proud of it
  • LoulanRE (2025-03-21 07:57): The endless stretches were stunning, and not deliberately landscaped either. Every household along the way was like this. Vitality, deeply felt the arrival of spring
  • Feng Juanlian Zhe Huaxiang (2025-03-21 08:19): You’ll eventually take some photos
  • LoulanRE reply @Feng Juanlian Zhe Huaxiang (2025-03-21 08:23): It was already beautiful, and there was so much more ahead that I didn’t even photograph
  • LoulanRE reply @Feng Juanlian Zhe Huaxiang (2025-03-21 08:27): Reminded me of that sea of flowers in Ghost of Tsushima
  • Feng Juanlian Zhe Huaxiang reply @LoulanRE (2025-03-21 08:27): Never played that
  • LoulanRE reply @Feng Juanlian Zhe Huaxiang (2025-03-21 08:30): Denji-kun, actually… I’ve never been to school either
  • Anonymous (2025-03-21 08:49): Four days on, three days off, Congratulations
  • LoulanRE reply @Anonymous (2025-03-21 09:56): No no no, thank my roommate, absolute carry
  • Chen (2025-03-21 10:12): That free?

[^1]: “Green-skin train” refers to China’s old-style slow trains with their distinctive green livery, an iconic symbol of Chinese railway culture before high-speed rail took over.
[^2]: “Clackety-clack” is the onomatopoeia for the rhythmic sound of train wheels rolling over rail joints.

entry.title: Gin and 1984
entry.content:
Had some gin

Reading 1984, it comes up again and again, the name alone was enough to spark curiosity

Pungent, invasive, almost unbearable, you can only gulp it down like medicine

Forty-three degrees burning at every moment, reminding you exactly where it is right now, face flushing, heating up, even wanting to tear up

The burning fades quickly, and now the world looks a bit easier, a bit more cheerful. Gradually I can smell it, musky, sharp and cool, and so intensely alcoholic you’d swear it was disinfectant ethanol

Mood soaring, but in a flash I thought of that devastating ending of 1984, savoring it over and over

In the end, I watched the film adaptation of 1984 together with gin

Happy National Day[^1] (lol)[^2]

** Comments**

  • abc (2024-10-01 22:58): Using war to maintain class hierarchy is…
  • Yaoxing (2024-10-02 19:53): Hello, my artist

Footnotes

[^1]: National Day, Chinese National Day, celebrated on October 1st, marking the founding of the People’s Republic of China.
[^2]: The irony of celebrating National Day while drinking Victory Gin, in Orwell’s 1984, Victory Gin is the cheap, harsh liquor the Party provides to the populace of Oceania. It symbolizes the bleak, controlled existence under totalitarian rule: a substance that dulls rather than pleasures, offered as a perverse “reward”by the very system that oppresses its people. Reading 1984 and drinking gin on China’s National Day carries an unmistakable layer of dark humor.

entry.title: Surprise! Heard My Favorite Band toe's Song at Rehearsal
entry.content:
Walking past the sports field, I suddenly heard the intro to goodbye[^1]

From vaguely familiar to instantly dizzy with surprise

In a melody only we know

That sense of belonging, tears almost coming out

The best part of this song is the drums, the drummer pouring everything in, riding the rhythm with swagger, launching tidal waves of beats

“When a Drummer Enters Their Flow State”?live is the peak
