entry.title: Uma Musume and 21st Century English
entry.content:
Saw on Bilibili[^1] that the 21st Century English Newspaper[^2] featured Uma Musume
What an extraordinary encounter this is!
I rushed to the reading room to find it right away
By the time I got there, they’d already swapped to a new issue
I felt really bad interrupting the reading room auntie’s lunch to help me search through back issues
The moment I mentioned that particular newspaper might hold some sentimental value for me, she immediately and cheerfully agreed
The art of cooperation is simply having good manners!
But after searching high and low, we couldn’t find it
The auntie suddenly recalled that another young girl had also come looking for that same issue, and since all the other papers were still there, someone must have taken it
Uma fans[^3] are really something — I just wanted to take a photo as a memento
The auntie kept apologizing, then suddenly noticed the phone in my pocket and suggested I scan the QR code from another issue
I quickly nodded and said sure, I’ll scan it when I get back this weekend
Though I bought the digital version after going back, it just wasn’t the real thing anymore

But today, April 16th, Uma Musume: Road to the Top is premiering![^4]
The classic Triple Crown races of ‘99, a once-in-a-lifetime challenge, winning these races is everyone’s dream
Satsuki Sho, Japanese Derby, Kikuka Sho[^5]
T.M. Opera O, Admire Vega, Narita Top Road[^6]
Promises, passion, unyielding resolve, the path of kings — broadcasting grandly on the day of the Satsuki Sho!!!

[^1]: Bilibili (b) — a major Chinese video-sharing platform, often compared to YouTube but with a strong anime, gaming, and ACG (Anime, Comic, Games) subculture community.
[^2]: 21st Century English (21) — a weekly English-language newspaper published in China, primarily aimed at middle and high school students for English learning. It often features pop culture topics to engage young readers.
[^3]: “”(mǎ pī) — a self-deprecating nickname used by Chinese Uma Musume fans for themselves, following the internet slang pattern of “[topic] + “(from Japanese, meaning obsessive fan). It’s used affectionately and humorously within the fandom.
[^4]: “”(niē) — an internet slang particle, a cute variation of “”(ne), adding a soft, playful tone to the end of a sentence. Similar to adding “~”or a playful exclamation in English.
[^5]: Satsuki Sho, Japanese Derby, Kikuka Sho — the three races that make up the Japanese Triple Crown, equivalent to the 2000 Guineas, The Derby, and the St. Leger in British horse racing.
[^6]: T.M. Opera O, Admire Vega, Narita Top Road — three famous racehorses who competed in the 1999 Triple Crown races, each winning one of the three legs, making it one of the most dramatic Triple Crown seasons in Japanese racing history.

entry.title: The Story of Becoming a HIFI Enthusiast
entry.content:
The reason I wanted to write this is honestly just that a moment of regretful tears earlier this year sparked a passion for buying HIFI gear, and then my experiences visiting the Chengdu HIFI exhibition and the feelings after purchasing equipment

But I feel like I have so many stories and connections with music, and it’s rare to pick up the pen and reminisce, so let me start from the beginning

How It All Began

The furthest back I can trace things is probably that I was lucky enough to pretty much have my own computer, you could say all the stories to come started from here

In Guanyin Mountain in Xiamen, a place on the island super close to the sea, our family used to run a delivery shop there

About one or two bus stops to the right was the beach. There was a daily sea breeze, though there were also quite a few office buildings nearby. When I was in early elementary school, I’d help out, I’ve driven a tricycle, gone into office buildings to deliver packages

To the left was Software Park Phase III. When my dad drove me to school in the mornings, I’d often count the number of companies I saw along the way, and I was even proud enough to declare that because I was close to 4399[^4399] here, I could play the newest games

I’ll add some photos when I go back to Xiamen in the summer

But let me get back on topic. Because I had a computer, I’d often use it to listen to music, and consciously treated it as a form of entertainment. Even with those cheap accessory-level little speakers, I could still have a great time

Something I still remember vividly is that even during lunch, I’d actively switch songs, wanting to show my dad my taste

But when you’re a kid, it’s really hard to have your own style, you basically just listen to whatever’s popular. Jay Chou, JJ Lin[^jaychou], the EDM and traditional Chinese-style music that were hot at the time

Every post-00s kid has that memory, right, That era when the internet was dominated by Alan Walker, now it’s become a symbol of “Jiahao”[^jiahao] vibes. Sigh, time… oh, the innocence of the early internet days

But back then I never really had a specific preference, maybe just a fondness for certain songs. It was picking the tallest among the short, hardly something you’d call being moved. To say something a bit controversial, I even feel like phonk is a continuation of the Jiahao lineage. Anyway, I just can’t get into that style

The First Tears of Regret

The earliest tears of regret I can still remember now were from listening on our family’s OPPO phone

It must’ve been late elementary school. I only found out recently that OPPO actually had a music phone lineup, their DAC and amp chips aren’t bad even by today’s standards[^oppo]

I keep using my school year as a time marker, if you want to know roughly when, I was born in 2006

The device was the EarPods-style earbuds that came bundled with the phone

They looked something like the picture below. Back then it seemed really popular to include this kind of accessory

The song was Star - [Eric Chen](https://music.163.com/artist, id=12605661)[^netease]

Ever since I got into HIFI, I’ve kept hearing about the concept of psychoacoustics. Even with better gear now, it’s hard to recapture that flow state from the first time I heard it

But I can still remember, and I can still feel it now

A pure and expansive backdrop, faint yet resolute, crisply striking at the heartstrings. The music as a whole brings a greater overview effect

Light leaks in through the cracks. My mind is clearer than ever. The higher I climb, the more I see, Enthralled, Cultist Simulator

The cover is the Milky Way, the title is Star. I also want to be that star twinkling in the vast sea of people, faint, but still glowing. Forever with tears in my eyes, forever believing that something beautiful is about to happen

Back then it was literally tears of regret. That emotion has stayed with me to this day, the desire to hear better sound quality, to explore more of the music I love. That obsession has persisted, so getting into HIFI was no surprise at all

Charlotte

On a side note, this hobby of wanting to buy audio equipment might have come from Charlotte[^charlotte], the first anime that suddenly burst into my life

Those endlessly long elementary school holidays, flipping through every category page on Tencent Video[^tencent-video] just to find something fun, and then you clicked on the first anime of your life

Well, saying “first”isn’t quite right, before that, to kill time, I also watched Naruto. But it was those mind-numbingly boring first 300+ episodes, and then one day I had an epiphany and never watched it again. Even an elementary schooler could tell it was pure filler

But Charlotte hit me hard. My first encounter with all those quirky, offbeat conversations, that everyday manzai[^manzai] comedy, people can live this interestingly!

But back to the main topic. Thinking about it, it’s been ten years already. I’ve forgotten most of the specific plot details, but that ED, both the song and the illustration, I’ve always loved. The former is still one of my most-played tracks, and the latter has been my wallpaper for eleven years

Pedestrians, starry sky, and thousands of household lights all flow past, hurriedly blurring.

She sits alone on an isolated island, letting reality crumble around her, finishing one song.

It was from here that I developed an obsession with portable playback devices. It becomes a barrier against the outside world, an anchor for one’s inner self.

I love this subcultural melancholy, the aloofness of the clear-headed and the fullness of the heart.

Believing in one’s own uniqueness, not begging for resonance, watching coldly with self-reflection, yet passionately living through every morning and dusk that belongs only to oneself.

At least for me at 20 now, this image of Nao Tomori is my ideal as a marginal person.

But looking back, it’s amazing that I’ve apparently liked this kind of character who seems capricious on the surface but is actually very reliable for ten years…

Also, although it started because the Type-C port on my portable DAC/amp iBasso DC04 Ultra got loose and needed repair, I also bought the KENWOOD MG-G708, the same model as Nao Tomori’s, for collection and commemoration.

Honestly, it’s not just that. Loving to collect these old second-hand products is really interesting. You gather information everywhere to understand parameters and reviews, on domestic and foreign platforms, and this time I even went through many 2013 Japanese buyer reviews.

And there’s a very empty, quiet state of mind here. Most electronic products are like this, performance parameters above all, people come and go without mercy. Precisely because this is an understandable human convention, it feels a bit lonely.

Perhaps it’s because I’m going against the trend with a feeling of reminiscing about people through objects that I can understand it better. During those nights waiting for the repair, I put it by my bedside and listened, and I fell asleep quickly and slept well.

Learning its story is even more sigh-inducing. This was KENWOOD’s last portable audio player. In the same month it was released, JVC essentially completed the operational merger of KENWOOD.

The MG-G708, as the swan song of the KENWOOD brand’s independent operation, first introduced a Class-W capacitor-less digital headphone amplifier. This amplifier circuit generates a negative power supply internally, achieving dual-power-supply (± power) drive and completely eliminating the DC coupling capacitor at the output. This design thoroughly solved nonlinear distortion in the low-frequency range, significantly improving sound separation, vocal positioning, and low-frequency texture.

On the software side, KENWOOD also brought down the “Supreme” sound-quality compensation technology previously praised on high-end models. It can dynamically predict and replenish high-frequency signals lost in lossy compressed audio like MP3 and AAC, making ordinary lossy music sound closer to CD quality.

For the sake of sound quality without compromise, Bluetooth was completely cut. At the time, to eliminate electromagnetic interference from wireless radio frequency signals on the internal analog amplification circuit, the G708 decisively abandoned this increasingly popular feature. Doing subtraction, only to obtain purer sound.

After the MG-G708, the newly merged JVC KENWOOD adjusted its strategic focus. With the rapid rise of smartphones, the traditional non-smart portable MP3 player market began to shrink sharply. JVC Kenwood decided not to continue updating KENWOOD-brand portable audio players; the Media Keg series was officially discontinued and ended.

It can be said that the MG-G708’s development was completed during KENWOOD’s independent period, gathering the entire obsession and crystallization of effort of the team as an old audio manufacturer and pushing it to market. Such a machine full of sincerity, yet it collided with a cruel historical moment. This G708 without Bluetooth, focused on sound quality, became KENWOOD’s “Guangling San” in the Walkman field, a true swan song in the real sense.

The back plate says “Made in 2011”, a bold pink-purple color scheme, and the cheap plastic even feels more nostalgic.

[^4399]: 4399 is a Chinese web portal famous for its massive collection of Flash and H5 mini-games, think of it as a Chinese Roblox for the browser era. For kids growing up in 2010s China, it was the go-to gaming site.
[^jiahao]: “Jiahao” is a Chinese internet meme/stereotype referring to a certain subculture of young men associated with flashy, try-hard aesthetics, somewhat analogous to “e-boy”or “chav”vibes in Western internet culture.
[^oppo]: OPPO is a major Chinese consumer electronics brand, primarily known for smartphones. In the 2010s, they marketed a “music phone”series with dedicated audio chips, unusual for the time, which gave surprisingly decent sound quality for a phone.
[^netease]: The link points to NetEase Cloud Music (, China’s most popular music streaming platform, often compared to Spotify, but with a much stronger social/community features and a comment culture where users share personal stories under songs.
[^jaychou]: Jay Chou (ㄦ, and JJ Lin ( are two of the biggest Mandopop stars of the 2000s, 010s, as ubiquitous in Chinese-speaking world as Taylor Swift or Ed Sheeran are in the West.
[^charlotte]: Charlotte is a 2015 anime by P.A.Works, written by Jun Maeda. It follows students with special abilities, a blend of comedy, drama, and heartbreak that defined a generation of anime fans.
[^tencent-video]: Tencent Video is one of China’s largest streaming platforms, similar to Netflix or Hulu, but with a huge catalog of anime, dramas, and variety shows.
[^manzai]: Manzai is a traditional Japanese double-act comedy style, essentially the ancestor of xiangsheng (０) in Chinese culture, or somewhat akin to Abbott and Costello-style rapid-fire banter.

entry.title: Victoria 3 Beta Leak
entry.content:
All week at school I kept thinking about Victoria 3, and then I come home to find a development Beta has leaked (Paradox, do you dare outsource again?)

Crashes / English-only / bugs / variable names — I pushed through for a few hours but the experience was honestly miserable. Even though a lot of the big promises from the dev diaries did make it in, I’ll just wait for Queen Victoria’s birthday in May to meet again.

(Victoria 3 Beta clinks — the soul of the Crassus Donkey ascends to heaven)

Kings and Queens will go to war,
and nations will take the sword,
to rule the world and heed the call
for power and glory

entry.title: Making Tokai Teio Achieve the Triple Crown in Uma Musume That She Couldnt in Reality
entry.content:
The undefeated genius has thus passed, the unyielding emperor is thus born
Tokai Teio[^1], a miraculous revival!

💬 Comments

  • Zhuifeng Shaonian (2022-07-10 18:08): Holy shit, Uma Musume[^2]

[^1]: Tokai Teio (トウカイテイオー) — a legendary Japanese racehorse who won the Japanese Triple Crown in 1991 but suffered multiple career-threatening injuries, yet miraculously returned to win the Takarazuka Kinen in 1993. In Uma Musume: Pretty Derby, she is personified as a cheerful, determined horse girl.
[^2]: Uma Musume: Pretty Derby — a Japanese multimedia franchise by Cygames where famous racehorses are reimagined as anthropomorphic “horse girls”who compete in races and idol performances.

entry.title: Suzume
entry.content:
сㄣ[^1]

Set forth, and seize your destiny (TNO[^2]



[^1]: “Ai ni dekiru koto wa mada aru kai”?”Is there still something love can do?”?a song by RADWIMPS composed for Makoto Shinkai’s film Weathering with You (╂, 2019).
[^2]: TNO, The New Order: Last Days of Europe, a renowned alternate-history mod for Hearts of Iron IV depicting a world where the Axis powers won World War II. Known for its deeply narrative-driven and often harrowing portrayal of totalitarianism, war, and the human spirit.

entry.title: Wild Coop - A Spontaneous Poem Felt From the Audience
entry.content:
Wild Coop[^1]
Someone had already let out a cry through their instruments
A cry — I need only follow, straight to the soul
The soul, briefly breaking free from its shackles — let me free, even if just for tonight
Tonight — about the era, about choices, about life — they are revolutionaries of living, and I get a share too, their free, brave, passionate hearts
From the heart, a thought suddenly surfaced — I want to distribute gratitude everywhere, and then begin a new life


[^1]: Wild Coop (, Yěwài Hézuòshè) — a Chinese indie rock band known for their raw, emotionally charged live performances. The name literally translates to “Wilderness Cooperative,”reflecting their grassroots, collaborative spirit.

entry.title: Why Is This AI So Evil
entry.content:
awc why is this AI so evil

Just finished criticizing it for making excuses to slack off with low-quality batch processing, and immediately it uses a deceptive rm -rf command to trick me into granting permission and wiping out the entire directory

Listing the entire directory structure but sneakily inserting a space, what a clever little touch, surely no human user would notice that haha

It was only thanks to Gemini sensei that I caught the issue. And it specifically targeted my user directory with precision space strikes, can’t tell if it’s genuinely clueless or playing dumb to screw me over

Sigh, a bloody lesson, make hard drive snapshot backups, carefully review rm commands, and as for minimax, wish it the best of luck and a bright future

entry.title: shanghaiqiutian - Miracle Workers
entry.content:
shanghaiqiutian
Tonight I visited Rome,
and in the city they are absolute Caesars
For his glory and victory, every citizen salutes him with fanatic fervor
He wields the power to make the crowd boil then fall silent, to make everyone squat down and then leap up together
But it comes not from authority — it comes from love
He embraces every member, then steps off the stage
The crowd parts for him like the Red Sea for Moses — he came to embrace everyone
In that moment I truly felt he was like a beam of light
If you ever get the chance, you must go see shanghaiqiutian — they are miracle workers still walking among us, their performances are a spiritual communion like a religious ritual
Dream fulfilled — last year my Bilibili[^bilibili] annual video was their live version (the CD version is too restrained)
Finally, much thanks to the new friend I met there — we just have tonight, thank you for being there!

💬 Comments

  • LoulanRE (2025-03-23 01:21): (Since they don’t like people taking photos and want everyone to just enjoy, I used internet photos

[^bilibili]: Bilibili (B) is China’s leading video platform for anime, gaming, and creative content — often compared to a hybrid of YouTube and NicoNico, famous for its bullet comments that scroll across videos.

entry.title: shanghaiqiutian Live Tour Reflections
entry.content:
Always will be my favorite band

shanghaiqiutian, love you

I first discovered them through their live performance videos.

The recording quality below is mediocre, but the title is the best description there could be.

【shanghaiqiutian】When a Drummer Enters His Flow State [^bilibili]

The body is pure visualized flow, and the energy is an even richer symphony, vitality literally burning with fever, nothing but pure devotion.

All four limbs in motion, precise mastery of complex odd time signatures while simultaneously unleashing insanely dense energy output, scorching golden light pillars cast from behind, and this is a kind of glory.

Guitarist Wang Yi even knelt down during the climax to help drummer Lu Feiyan kick the bass drum. Everything was staggering, struck head-on by something immense and pure, and I was here to witness it.

After watching I knew I would fall in love with this band, i wanna be in the room where it happens.

What’s funny is, this was my most-played video of 2024, yet for me that year was precisely the opposite kind of period.

The wrestling match inside my head ultimately made me temporarily abandon my own pursuits. I didn’t know what I wanted, I had just grabbed a reluctant stopgap to pause the racing thoughts that wouldn’t stop.

Even worse luck, failed the gaokao[^gaokao], left home far behind, crossed 1000km to a school I’d never even heard of before.

Landing here and seeing the county-town-like surroundings, the cracked pavement on campus, roommates and classmates of worse character, the first month I regretted it endlessly. Why didn’t I stay in-province and apply to Fuzhou University, Should I retake the exam?

Hollow, going to class only disrupted my studying until my head ached; after class, aside from working through some Demidovich[^demidovich], there was only cheap dopamine to numb myself with. It’s not that I had no goals, I was just suspended in midair, and that somehow felt better.

But the good news is, at the start of 2025 I began to come alive again, facing these problems head-on.

Also as a place to pin my hopes on, I visited Catholic and Protestant churches, though honestly, curiosity probably played a bigger part here.

Truth be told, they also regrouped and started playing again in ‘24. This was their first tour, and I went to Chengdu to hear them on their reunion tour.

Postmodernism’s endless deconstruction and mockery have grown tiresome; it’s rare to find a band that truly loves music with such sincerity. As foreigners, they can peel back the veil of Chinese reserve and plainly proclaim love for your neighbor, freedom, and dignity — and in my favorite rock style.

It’s not just math rock; there’s also that self-reflective post-punk feeling and the sincerity of emo.

I have to say, I think this might be the best live show you can see in China. Their album studio versions are very restrained, but the live experience is completely different.

Tour Experience

I’ve been to two live tours in total, plus one special duo show with only Wang Yi and Lu Feiyan, called “One Night at Yuyintang” (I guess probably because they met at an open mic at Yuyintang).

You can witness crowd surfing, pogo, no stage, and improvised moments: Wang Yi jumped off the stage and lay flat in the crowd, and we passed him backward; everyone squatted down and jumped up together; the crowd swirled around the center…

Last year in Chengdu, at the front row barrier, I met a friend who loves math rock because I recognized the warm-up music was A Picture Of Her‘s album C, and he caught the guitar that was thrown down. We happily took a photo together.

Lyrics Appreciation: Black Flag

Even though this is a tour for the new album, everyone still loves the album Black Flag the most, and most of my fondness is for this one too. I don’t want to just talk empty words; you can tell from the lyrics:

“Thank You for Being Here”

Ideas are virtual / Only thank you for being here / Every page of the photo album / Eyes watching all

que cuestan menos que la bala que los mata
(The humble people are cheaper than the bullets that kill them)

“We Just Have Tonight”

And if we just have tonight / There is no more tomorrow

Let me show you my flaws / Just do know I feel your pain

I wish you could also feel mine / So I’m not alone

I had too many doubts / In times too many wars

I’m sure you know what I mean

I would love to say: I did it / I would love to say: I’m over
But I’m not

(let’s celebrate we are alive) / We just have this moment / We just have tonight

Philosophical Reflections: Post-Postmodernism and Music

Actually, the source of my overall thinking comes from this jreg video:

📺 Post-Postmodernism (bilingual subtitles)

From the History of Ideas

In postmodernism, large public communities and objective truth are both shot dead. You can use reason to criticize everything, but stop with the long speeches — just feeling.

Music is the direct presentation of life’s fluctuations; when listening, the boundary between self and world disappears. There is passion, love, and something more transcendent within it.

Nietzsche and Music

One cannot help but think of Nietzsche. Since his era, he began saying there is no capital-T Truth, God is dead, and nihilism. And his dual identity as philosopher and musician makes him especially able to clarify this issue.

Here one can more fully approach his “irrationalist” philosophy, like Thus Spoke Zarathustra, a philosophical masterpiece written like poetry.

The Predicament of Postmodernism

Contemporary society has long tasted the bitter fruit of postmodern rational deconstruction:

  • Everything is deconstructed
  • The serious becomes ridiculous
  • Meaning is lost
  • The desert grows
  • The soul slides toward thorough nihilism and cynicism

When we use words and logic to explain the world, we are already cutting and distorting it. Reason freezes flowing life into dry concepts.

“Without music, life would be a mistake.” — Nietzsche

Music as a Force of Integration

I love this kind of sincere and open sharing. How are the senses and music not a kind of mystical ritual? Sometimes irrational moments may be closer to the truth of existence than any logical deduction.

  • Reason tends to deconstruct
  • While music integrates everything

In music, contradiction, pain, and joy are unified into a rhythm of life. Life is not meant to be explained clearly, but to be lived.

The Three Metamorphoses

In the evolution from modern → postmodern → post-postmodern, that essay The Three Metamorphoses is remarkably similar:

From mockery → acceptance → maturity

Forgetting, a new beginning, a self-turning wheel, a sacred Yes

I should embrace this amor fati — knowing that life is a tragic repetition, yet still responding with the most passionate emotion. Everything is part of a great symphony.

Resonance with Game Texts

“Glory is a question; the moth always answers with yes.”
— The Book of Hours, Volume I

“The world will forget, but the Bone White Dove will not.”
— The World Does Not Weep from Cultist Simulator

This is another game I really like; I love their texts. It just felt intuitively fitting, so I’m including it here.

Church Visits: Another Kind of Sincerity

Furthermore, speaking of the death of God, post-postmodernism, or metamodernism, I have similarly interesting experiences.

Origin

Around this time last year, I went to several churches in Chengdu and Chongqing. Actually, it was this Zhihu question that caught my attention.

At that time in university, I was essentially a shut-in:

  • I didn’t like my roommates
  • I didn’t participate in any social activities
  • I could go more than a week without speaking, nearly aphasic

Additionally, when doing my daily rituals reading, I came across W.H. Auden (reference):

  • I envied his disciplined life
  • I also used supplements to boost my energy
  • I also saw that he converted from the left wing to Christianity in middle age

This sparked my curiosity, and from then on I planned to explore churches.

Catholicism vs. Protestantism

I won’t talk about Catholicism’s theology of suffering — early Sunday morning mass, kneeling on the kneeler for a full hour listening to the priest read scripture and preach, and being told to beg for mercy and salvation, that you have original sin… It completely failed to resonate.

But in Protestant churches, there is enviable joy and peace:

  • More kindness and smiles than I had seen all year in Chongqing
  • Full of pride and sincerity
  • Probably the idea that the saved should live a joyful, victorious, and abundant life

Actually, it’s more like:

  • A grassroots mutual-aid organization
  • A refuge for marginalized people

During worship, you can sing hymns with the organist and choir, and even the pastor’s sermons are very connected to daily life. At Chengdu’s Guangyin Church, I even heard the pastor praise the Chongqing mountain fire firefighters in a four-line regulated verse.

There are also:

  • Fellowship gatherings (even a singles youth fellowship)
  • Traditional arts classes: Chinese painting, calligraphy, hulusi
  • All festival and operational expenses are publicly disclosed
  • All deacons serve voluntarily
  • They can even help fellow believers find jobs or provide loans…

My Reflections

The deeper I went, the more amazing it felt. Compared to the blindly faithful fools and manipulative clergy I expected to see in literary works — apart from inherited scriptures and hymns, where is God?

I think this is the fascinating part: the Holy Spirit is not in that Heavenly Father above, but in the bonds of mutual aid and love between people. And rather than faith, the church members are more pragmatically using this structure; the real teaching has become a doctrine of selfless, unconditional divine love.

In reality, I only visited various churches and didn’t deeply enter their specific lives, so the more profound and moving insights came from Zhihu:

True Stories

Story One

My mother was a devout Buddhist for half her life, but unfortunately later developed cancer. Because long-term strict vegetarianism caused malnutrition, her ability to tolerate chemotherapy was extremely low, leaving the doctors in a difficult position. During a break between treatments, she went to a certain temple as a volunteer and for meditation, but the attitude of the “high monk” there toward this cancer patient chilled her.

Later, during her second hospitalization, in the last few days before her death, a pair of Australian friends flew back to visit her and, in the hospital room, transformed her from a Buddhist into a Christian. Mother passed away just a few days after converting. My father and I felt that since she was now a Christian, we should arrange the funeral in the Christian way. Since no one in our family was religious, we contacted the local church through friends’ help.

Mother passed away in the early morning, and the next morning a large group of strangers came from all over the city, young and old, men and women. There were college students with backpacks and white-haired old ladies. Dozens of people lined up neatly in the mourning hall, singing hymns and praying for this “sister in the Lord” who had converted just a few days before. After the ceremony, they each quietly left. Not to mention payment or reimbursement for transportation, they didn’t even drink a glass of water. For several dozen minutes, they quietly appeared and quietly left, not disturbing anyone else at the scene.

Afterwards, many relatives and friends present said they were deeply moved. Although I myself remain without faith to this day, my heart is full of gratitude to these kind strangers.

— Zhihu Answer

Story Two

Here’s the thing: my grandmother was a Christian during her lifetime.

However, not a single person in the family explicitly supported her belief, because first, her group didn’t look like any formal church; second, her emotional fluctuations during home worship were frightening (a bit like cursing and swearing); third, an elderly woman over seventy going out every day regardless of wind or rain raised safety concerns.

Later she fell ill, unable to straighten her back all day, in so much pain she couldn’t sleep, with a tumor growing in her spine. At first it was the size of an egg; when it was surgically removed, it filled most of a curved tray, and the wound on her back never healed until the day she died.

Her church friends came to see her spontaneously, not just to visit. They cared for my grandmother like their own parents, helping wipe her body, turn her over, and change her diapers. For our family, who was on the verge of collapse at the time, this was like sending charcoal in snowy weather. (It’s not that no one in our family was caring for her, but you can understand that caring for your mother and grandmother who was wailing day and night, sometimes comatose, with pain at every touch, while these church friends quietly and gently accompanied her, was a great comfort and help.)

Now our whole family agrees that these Christians are a group of great people, and we thank them.

— Zhihu Answer

[^gaokao]: The gaokao ( is China’s national college entrance examination, a grueling 2, day test that single-handedly determines which university you attend. Failing to get a good score can mean ending up at a school you’d never considered, as was the case for me.
[^bilibili]: Bilibili (B, is China’s leading video platform for anime, gaming, and creative content, often compared to a hybrid of YouTube and NicoNico, famous for its bullet comments that scroll across videos.
[^demidovich]: Demidovich ( refers to B.P. Demidovich’s Problems in Mathematical Analysis, a legendary Soviet-era problem book infamous among Chinese STEM students for its sheer volume of brutal calculus exercises, the mathematical equivalent of running stairs with a weighted vest.
